question.text: Which form must be submitted by an individual who does not have a PAN and is applying for one for the first time?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Form 49A is the application form for allotment of a new PAN (Permanent Account Number) by Indian citizens and Indian entities. It is submitted to NSDL or UTIITSL, which act as PAN service agencies on behalf of the Income Tax Department.
**The Trap:** Form 60 is a declaration to be submitted by a person who does not have a PAN and enters into specified transactions — it is NOT an application for PAN. Form 61 is a declaration by a person who has agricultural income and does not have PAN. Form 15G is for declaring nil tax liability to avoid TDS. None of these are PAN application forms.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The distinction between Form 49A (PAN application), Form 60 (declaration when PAN unavailable), and Form 61 (agricultural income declaration) is tested in NISM Series V-A and X-A.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client does not have PAN, help them apply through Form 49A. In the interim, for eligible micro investments, accept Form 60 as a declaration. But always encourage PAN application as it will be needed for most financial transactions.
